title: Cross-Embodiment Representations
description: Keep in Survey: camera-space and latent-action papers solve important components, but our open decision is which representation to test on existing HOI/UMI data first.
summary: Survey-stage track for the Notion representation ideas: decide which action/state space can transfer across robot hands, arms, camera setups, and HOI demonstrations without collapsing into embodiment-specific joint values.

details.text: Dexterous-hand world model idea: represent action using fingertip camera pose or camera-frame geometry instead of robot-specific joint values. The strongest direct reference is camera-frame action unification with calibration-free dataset conversion, which is highly aligned with cross-embodiment pretraining.

links.label: Unify Robot Actions in Camera Frame

details.text: LAPA / DRO-style idea: learn latent actions or object-centric transformations from videos, then map to robot actions with small real-robot data. This is relevant to internet-scale data but must be checked against loss of contact and force semantics.

details: Random Thought entries folded here: hand as graph/tree/chains, cable-driven analogies, eigengrasp / PCA action space, active vision to reduce occlusion, RGB+XYZ as an image-like representation, point-cloud resampling for balanced attention, color-space effects on VLA policy, and object-centric contact/heatmap vs robot-centric joint values.

details.text: Depth/geometry augmentation matters because several Notion entries point at depth, point clouds, camera pose, and object state as the bridge between human video and robot action. Camera Depth Models, VGGT, FoundationStereo, and point tracking should be surveyed as representation tools, not only perception baselines.

Notion migration addendum 2026-05-31: point-cloud/image alignment, RGB+XYZ representation, point-cloud resampling, hand graph/tree/chains, eigengrasp/PCA action spaces, active vision for occlusion, color-space effects on VLA policy, and camera-frame fingertip actions are all retained as representation candidates.

risks_decisions: Need one comparison table that separates action representation, observation/state representation, calibration requirement, supported embodiments, and downstream task fit.
Latent action methods may scale data, but can hide geometry and contact. Camera-frame/action-state methods are more interpretable but require extrinsics or a calibration pipeline.
Eigengrasp and graph-hand abstractions are attractive for dexterous hands but may be too hand-specific for mobile or bimanual arm manipulation.
Do not create a new active research track until this survey picks one representation with a clear data source and benchmark.

Survey disposition 2026-05-31: keep, because OC-VLA and camera-frame action papers cover the camera-space component, not the full cross-embodiment representation choice across hands, arms, HOI videos, and robot trajectories.
Next executable analysis: compare camera-frame delta pose, object/contact heatmap, latent action, and RGB+XYZ state on one existing HOI/UMI task, then nominate one for a baseline.
Archive standalone ObsVLA/OC-VLA duplication, but keep this card as the live decision point for representation selection.
The migration audit found no reason to archive the representation cluster; archive only standalone paper duplicates such as OC-VLA/EgoVLA, while keeping the cross-embodiment decision matrix live.
